claim: The zodiac section's anomalously low entropy (3.7149 bits, lowest of all sections) reflects a label-only register drawn from a vocabulary of fewer than 200 distinct word types â€” a much smaller effective vocabulary than other sections â€” producing a Zipf exponent significantly above the corpus-wide value of 0.8946.
evidence_for: Zodiac section has only 1,297 words across 12 folios, consistent with repeated short labels around circular diagrams
Entropy 3.7149 is 0.19 bits below the text-only section and 0.15 bits below the herbal section, a large difference for a corpus with otherwise similar per-section entropies
Label registers in historical manuscripts (e.g., calendars, zodiac wheels) reuse a small set of names, month terms, and cardinal directions extensively
High-confidence belief [0.273] and [0.058] already partially support label-oriented low-entropy encoding in zodiac
evidence_against: Low entropy could alternatively reflect a different cipher key applied to zodiac text rather than a different register
12 folios is a small sample; low entropy might be a sampling artifact
If zodiac labels encode personal names (e.g., names around the naked-figure rings), name distributions are hard to predict statistically
test: Compute the Zipf exponent (log-log linear regression of rank vs frequency) separately for the zodiac section corpus and compare to the manuscript-wide exponent (0.8946). Also compute type-token ratio (unique words / total words) for zodiac vs all other sections.
test_metric: Zodiac-section Zipf exponent and type-token ratio versus corpus-wide values
test_threshold: If zodiac Zipf exponent > 1.15 (versus corpus 0.8946) and zodiac type-token ratio < 0.25 (indicating heavy repetition), label-register encoding is confirmed; if exponent < 1.0 or type-token ratio > 0.40, refute

tests_run.test: compute the zipf exponent (log-log linear regression of rank vs frequency) separately for the zodiac
tests_run.details: Observed Zipf exponent: 0.8946 (R²=0.9084). Natural language expected: ~1.0. Fit quality: moderate.

reasoning: A label register would produce distinctively different Zipf statistics from running prose or cipher text. Confirming this would justify treating the zodiac section as a separate decipherment target with a restricted vocabulary, potentially providing a crib for the broader cipher.
